what_it_does: Defines a systemd timer that triggers a periodic, hourly, persistent incremental refresh of a company ledger to keep the structural ledger from going stale.
observations: Persistent=true set so missed triggers run after downtime
Description explicitly calls it an incremental refresh for a structural ledger
No explicit Unit= or [Service] section; relies on implicit service pairing
standouts: Persistent=true ensures catch-up after missed intervals
Implicit pairing with a same-base-name service unit
concerns: No explicit Unit= reference, so correctness depends on a matching company-ledger-refresh.service existing
Hourly schedule with no AccuracySec or RandomizedDelaySec could cluster load
If the corresponding service is missing, the timer will activate but fail
No failure/notification handling visible in this file
notes: A straightforward persistent hourly systemd timer that assumes a companion service handles the actual ledger refresh.
questions: What command or logic does the associated service execute?
What is the 'structural ledger' and where is its state stored?
Does the matching company-ledger-refresh.service unit exist?
How are refresh failures detected or reported?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it schedules an hourly persistent timer to keep the ledger fresh.
intention_signals: The file is trying to guarantee that the company ledger is refreshed at least once every hour, including after system downtime, by using a persistent systemd timer.
contribution: Provides a recurring, persistent hourly schedule for a ledger refresh, contributing a reliable time-based trigger and recovery-after-downtime behavior to the system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a systemd timer unit file that schedules an hourly persistent activation of a company ledger refresh service.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to keep a company structural ledger from going stale by triggering a refresh every hour, even if the system was down at the scheduled time.
